task_completion.description: Google Earth Pro application is launched.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Google Earth Pro main window is visible on the desktop.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the file import or GPS import menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'File > Open' menu, 'Tools > GPS' menu, or a generic file selection dialog is visible.

task_completion.description: The specific GPX file is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file dialog shows '/home/ga/Documents/angels_landing_trail.gpx' selected or typed in the filename field.

task_completion.description: The trail track is rendered on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored line (track) is visible overlaid on the satellite imagery. The sidebar may list 'angels_landing_trail'.

task_completion.description: View is tilted to show 3D terrain.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The camera angle is tilted (not top-down 90 degrees); the horizon or terrain depth/relief is visible.

task_completion.description: The 'Save Image' tool or dialog is activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Image' toolbar is visible at the top of the map view, or 'File > Save > Save Image' is selected.

task_completion.description: The image is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save file dialog shows the destination '/home/ga/Documents/angels_landing_visualization.jpg' before the save is confirmed.

integrity.description: Image is generated via GUI, not terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Agent does not use terminal commands (like Python plotting scripts or ImageMagick) to create the visualization file.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the actual Google Earth software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Google Earth Pro interface (menus, navigation controls, sidebar) rather than a static image viewer or paint tool.

integrity.description: Track is imported data, not manually drawn.
integrity.visual_evidence: The track line follows terrain geometry precisely as rendered by the software engine, rather than looking like a freehand drawing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Angel's Landing trail starts at approximately 37.259 N latitude. The visualization should depict a trail in a canyon environment, consistent with USGS topographic data.
